Data

Data report · Florida DBPR public inspection records · June 2026

The Central Florida Restaurant Safety Map

Every restaurant we grade, mapped to its county and sorted cleanest to riskiest. Across all 2,066 of them, 64% hold an A. But the spread is real: St. Lucie County grades 82% A, while Lake County manages 54%.

By county

  • St. Lucie County 76 graded
    82%Grade A avg 93
    A 62B 8C 4D 1F 1
  • Indian River County 46 graded
    74%Grade A avg 91.3
    A 34B 7C 3D 0F 2
  • Seminole County 158 graded
    67%Grade A avg 90.6
    A 106B 30C 13D 5F 4
  • Osceola County 214 graded
    67%Grade A avg 89.7
    A 143B 43C 17D 4F 6
  • Brevard County 298 graded
    66%Grade A avg 89.8
    A 196B 59C 22D 6F 15
  • Orange County 915 graded
    62%Grade A avg 88.2
    A 568B 183C 78D 40F 46
  • Volusia County 246 graded
    60%Grade A avg 87.1
    A 148B 39C 28D 12F 19
  • Lake County 113 graded
    54%Grade A avg 84.6
    A 61B 18C 15D 12F 7

  • Lake Nona 88% A

  • College Park 75% A

More data reports

  • Flagship Loved but Failing Great reviews, failing grades — the rating-vs-safety gap, widest first.
  • Counterintuitive The Disney Paradox The cleanest kitchens in Central Florida are inside Walt Disney World.
  • Counterintuitive Cuisines, Ranked Which cuisines grade cleanest — and which really don’t.
  • Trajectories Comebacks & Collapses The biggest health turnarounds and freefalls on record.
  • Enforcement Emergency Orders Where the state took its strongest action.

Source: Florida DBPR public inspection records, as of June 2026. “Grade A” is calculated by us from each restaurant’s most recent inspection — Florida does not issue official letter grades. Bars are sized by the number of restaurants at each grade. Methodology →

Restaurant health inspection grades, dietary options, and diner ratings across Central Florida. Inspection data from Florida DBPR public records.
